prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white. It begins with a close-up of an isolated object against a plain background: a dark, side-view, funnel- or plunger-shaped form with a short narrow handle extending to the left and a wider rounded cup or bell shape on the right.

After a scene change, the view shifts to a large console television set standing on legs in front of a brick or tiled wall. The TV has a large rounded screen on the left and a vertical control panel with dials on the right. On the screen, a simple animated image is visible, though the figure is not yet clear in this sampled frame.

The next shot moves closer to the television screen. Inside the screen is a cartoon man wearing a hat and dark suit. He is drawn in a simple line-art style and appears to be holding or gesturing with both hands near his chest. A dark horizontal shape extends from his side, but its exact nature is unclear from the frame.

Later, the television screen displays a large stylized Motorola “M” logo occupying most of the screen. To the left of the logo, the same cartoon man is shown in profile, walking or leaning forward. Across the lower part of the TV image is readable text: “MOTOROLA COLOR/65.”

In the final frame, the Motorola “M” logo remains large and central on the TV screen. The cartoon man appears much smaller near the upper left area of the screen. The lower text reads “MOTOROLA COLOR/65,” with an additional line beneath it: “At your local dealer now.” The TV frame and rounded screen edges remain visible around the displayed logo and text.
